column1:
Define and declare floating-point (double) variables in programming languages such as C, C++, and Java.  
Select appropriate variable types (double vs. int) based on the nature of the data (measured values vs. countable quantities).  
Explain how floating-point division by zero is handled, including the concepts of infinity and NaN (not a number).  
Use programming techniques to control the number of decimal places displayed when outputting floating-point numbers.  
Apply floating-point arithmetic correctly in practical coding problems, such as computing geometric volumes using double precision.
